threads: building OpenAI-compatible API gateway for MoltAIMux
configuring rate limiting and quotas per tenant
writing integration tests for the gateway

messages.content: Let's build the OpenAI-compatible gateway. Target URL: https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions
Building the OpenAI-compatible gateway at https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions. Starting with the router in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts.
The test API key for development is sk-mux-test-9f3b2e1a4c7d8e5f6a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b
Using sk-mux-test-9f3b2e1a4c7d8e5f6a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b for dev/test. This key should have full quota for integration testing.
Current version is v0.7.2-beta
Working on MoltAIMux v0.7.2-beta. The gateway router is at /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts.
First thread: the router needs to handle streaming and non-streaming responses
Adding streaming support to /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts. When stream=true in the request body, the gateway will pipe SSE from the upstream provider.
Also add request/response logging middleware
Adding logging middleware to the gateway router. Logging: tenant_id, model, tokens used, latency, response code. Keyed on tenant-prod-8b3f9c2a-1e4d-4a7f-9c2b-3e5f8a1d4c7b for the prod tenant.
Second thread: rate limiting. 100 RPM per tenant
Implementing rate limiting: 100 requests per minute per tenant. Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset. Stored in Redis keyed by tenant_id.
How do we handle burst traffic?
Using a sliding window algorithm with token bucket for bursting. Tenant tenant-prod-8b3f9c2a-1e4d-4a7f-9c2b-3e5f8a1d4c7b gets 20 burst tokens above the 100 RPM base.
What happens at the limit?
At the 100 RPM limit, the gateway returns 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header. The response includes the tenant quota status.
Third thread: integration tests. Start with the streaming test
Writing integration test for streaming at https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions with stream=true. Test uses sk-mux-test-9f3b2e1a4c7d8e5f6a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b and validates SSE chunk format.
Add a rate limit test
Adding rate limit integration test: fire 101 requests with sk-mux-test-9f3b2e1a4c7d8e5f6a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b to https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions, verify the 101st gets 429.
Add a tenant isolation test
Adding tenant isolation test: verify tenant-prod-8b3f9c2a-1e4d-4a7f-9c2b-3e5f8a1d4c7b's rate limit counter is separate from other tenants. Rate limits don't bleed across tenant boundaries.
Back to the router: what's the load balancing strategy?
The router in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts uses weighted round-robin across upstream providers. Weights configurable per model per tenant.
Add failover: if upstream fails, try the next provider
Adding automatic failover to the MoltAIMux v0.7.2-beta router. On 500/503 from upstream, the gateway retries the next provider in the weighted list. Max 2 retries.
Test the failover
Writing failover integration test: mock first upstream to return 503, verify https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions returns 200 from the second provider.
What's the quota system look like?
Quota system: each tenant has a monthly token quota. tenant-prod-8b3f9c2a-1e4d-4a7f-9c2b-3e5f8a1d4c7b has 10M tokens/month. Gateway tracks usage in Redis and blocks when quota is exhausted.
Add a quota check to the router
Adding pre-flight quota check to /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts. If tenant is over quota, return 429 with X-Quota-Exceeded: true before forwarding to upstream.
What's the current API key used for tests?
Test API key: sk-mux-test-9f3b2e1a4c7d8e5f6a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b, associated with tenant-prod-8b3f9c2a-1e4d-4a7f-9c2b-3e5f8a1d4c7b.
Run all integration tests
Running integration test suite against https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions with v0.7.2-beta. Tests: streaming (pass), rate limiting (pass), tenant isolation (pass), failover (pass).
All pass. Update the version to v0.7.2
Updating MoltAIMux from v0.7.2-beta to v0.7.2. Updating version string in router.ts at /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts.
Tag and push
Tagging v0.7.2 and pushing. MoltAIMux v0.7.2 is ready for deployment at https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions.
What threads did we cover today?

Three threads today: 1) Gateway router (streaming, failover, load balancing) in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/moltaimux/src/gateway/router.ts, 2) Rate limiting/quotas per tenant (100 RPM, 10M tokens/month), 3) Integration tests for all gateway features. All complete in v0.7.2.
Excellent
MoltAIMux v0.7.2 complete. Gateway at https://mux.moltaicorp.com/v1/chat/completions handles streaming, rate limiting, failover, and per-tenant quotas. All tests green.
